name: ForwardLane Advisor (DialogTool)
summary: Legacy Node.js/Express financial advisor platform with IBM Watson dialog integration, Sequelize/MySQL ORM, portfolio management, alerts, compliance, recommendation engine, and MorningStar fund integration. Originally built ~2016 on Node 0.10 (IBM Watson Dialog Tool fork), recently patched to Node 20 with security hardening. Extensive domain model (~40 Sequelize models) covering clients, portfolios, instruments, strategies, recommendations, and alerts.

score_rationale.revenue_potential: Legacy internal tool for ForwardLane advisors. Revenue is indirect—supports advisor workflows but unlikely to generate new revenue without major modernization. The domain models (portfolios, recommendations, alerts) hold business logic value.
score_rationale.strategic_value: Contains core ForwardLane domain knowledge: client segmentation, portfolio analysis, recommendation engine, compliance workflows, alert systems. This IP is strategically important even if the codebase is legacy. Could inform a modern rebuild.
score_rationale.completeness: Feature-rich: 20+ route modules covering admin, analysis, strategy, instruments, universes, recommendations, compliance, signals, relationships, clients, portfolios, alerts, cards, products, currency. 40+ DB models. Functional but dated.
score_rationale.urgency: Low urgency—recent security patches (Dockerfile SSH exfil removed, Node 20 upgrade, helmet/CORS hardening) reduced immediate risk. Not actively developed. Can sit as-is while decisions are made.
score_rationale.effort_remaining: Massive modernization effort: Sequelize 3→7, Jade→modern frontend, Express patterns, test coverage unknown, many deprecated deps (node-uuid, request, xmldom). Realistically easier to extract domain logic and rebuild than to incrementally upgrade.

git_recent: f3e99a1 chore: upgrade Node.js 0.10 → v20 LTS, update Dockerfile + package.json engines
308f960 SECURITY: Remove SSH key exfiltration from Dockerfile
a35be55 docs: add comprehensive README with business overview and technical documentation

modernization: Upgrade Sequelize 3.x → 6.x+ (breaking changes in associations, scopes, migrations)
Replace Jade/Pug templates with modern frontend (React/Next.js or at minimum Pug)
Upgrade `watson-developer-cloud` → `ibm-watson` SDK or remove if Watson Dialog is sunset
Add proper environment config management (dotenv or similar, remove hardcoded paths)
Add ESLint + Prettier for code consistency
Add proper logging infrastructure (structured JSON logs, replace bunyan if desired)
Containerize properly: multi-stage Docker build, non-root user, health checks
Add CI/CD pipeline (tests, linting, security scanning)
Extract business logic from route handlers into service layer
deprecation_plan: Phase 1: Document all active users/integrations depending on this system
Phase 2: Identify which domain modules (recommendations, portfolios, alerts) are still business-critical vs unused
Phase 3: Extract critical business logic and Sequelize models into a shared library or API spec
Phase 4: Build modern replacement (TypeScript, modern ORM, proper API design) informed by extracted domain knowledge
Phase 5: Migrate data and switch over, decommission legacy app
Timeline suggestion: 3-6 months for full deprecation if actively staffed
security_vulnerabilities: CRITICAL (FIXED): Dockerfile previously fetched SSH private keys from external HTTP server with hardcoded bearer token—supply chain attack vector. Removed in commit 308f960.
HIGH: `trim` package v0.0.1 — known prototype pollution vulnerability (CVE-2020-7753)
HIGH: `sequelize` 3.x — multiple known CVEs, no longer maintained
MEDIUM: `minimatch` 3.x — ReDoS vulnerability in older versions
MEDIUM: `lodash` 4.17.4 — needs update to 4.17.21+ for prototype pollution fixes
MEDIUM: Cookie session without secure flag enforcement in production
LOW: `jade` 1.9 — deprecated and renamed to Pug, no security patches
LOW: Git dependency `fl-dialog` fetched via SSH from Bitbucket—ensure repo access is controlled
LOW: `file-manager-js` fetched from GitHub URL without version pinning
RECOMMENDATION: Run `npm audit` after install to get full CVE report; expect 50+ vulnerabilities
